symptoms.symptom_name: Continuous vomiting beginning within hours of chemotherapy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Temporal onset within hours of cytotoxic therapy is characteristic of acute CINV.
Strict temporal link to treatment and absence of infective features argue against gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to tolerate oral fluids or oral anti-emetic (vomited back)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Refractory chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting with dehydration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of the oral route means oral rescue is impossible and parenteral therapy is required.
Postural symptoms are a patient-reportable proxy for significant intravascular volume depletion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute kidney injury from volume depletion
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oliguria signals impending or established pre-renal AKI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound anorexia, altered taste (dysgeusia) and unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anorexia-cachexia with dysgeusia and progressive weight loss reflects the catabolic cancer state.
A single afebrile home reading lowers immediate probability but does not exclude neutropenic sepsis; threshold to escalate on any new fever stays low.
Absence of diarrhoea reduces the likelihood of an infective gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No headache, visual change or altered consciousness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of central features lowers, but does not exclude, a CNS cause of vomiting.
Compensatory tachycardia to volume depletion — a sealed clinician measurement, not available in chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Borderline hypotension (blood pressure ~95/60) with weak peripheral pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensated volume depletion; frank hypotension would indicate shock. Sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor skin turgor, delayed capillary refill (3s), dry cracked lips

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective dehydration signs requiring hands-on examination — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender, scaphoid abdomen with hypoactive bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of distension or a tense/tender abdomen argues against obstruction; requires in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cachectic habitus with temporal and shoulder-girdle muscle wasting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible skeletal muscle wasting is a hallmark of the cancer anorexia-cachexia syndrome; partly video-observable but graded on examination.

investigation_details.expected_result: Raised urea/creatinine (pre-renal AKI) and possible hypokalaemia from vomiting
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed AKI and dangerous hypokalaemia predisposing to arrhythmia.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute kidney injury from volume depletion
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Renal function and potassium can only be established by bloods, not by chat.

investigation_details.expected_result: Needed to exclude hypercalcaemia of malignancy as a cause of refractory vomiting
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed hypercalcaemia of malignancy, a treatable oncological emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood examination with absolute neutrophil count

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination / absolute neutrophil count
investigation_details.expected_result: Assesses neutropenia; a low ANC with any fever mandates emergency antibiotics
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed neutropenia and delayed recognition of neutropenic sepsis risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neutrophil status is a laboratory determination central to febrile-neutropenia risk.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Failed oral route with volume depletion
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Refractory chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting with acute dehydration
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Inability to keep fluids/anti-emetics down plus orthostatic symptoms and oliguria together indicate significant dehydration needing parenteral care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detect pre-renal AKI and hypokalaemia driving arrhythmia risk — done in-person/ED, not orderable by the AI.
Exclude hypercalcaemia of malignancy as a treatable cause of refractory vomiting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood examination / absolute neutrophil count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess neutropenia in an on-chemotherapy patient; underpins the low threshold for neutropenic-sepsis treatment if any fever develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine abdominal imaging for every case of vomiting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated without clinical suspicion of obstruction; the soft scaphoid non-tender abdomen argues against it and imaging should not be reflexively ordered.
